results.check: Member CLI Python syntax compilation and command discovery
Existing assignment-snapshot and authorized-baseline Git validation remains preserved
V1.7.5 task contract hash protects human_collaboration and submission_confirmation independently
human_collaboration none disables Grill but still requires human-submission-confirmation.yaml
init creates a not-prepared confirmation record and manifest summary
Validation is blocked before preparation and while awaiting the registered human owner
Explicit owner confirmation binds the current normalized body hash and personal stance
confirm, oppose, question, and reserve remain valid personal positions without Gate effect
A material main-output change after confirmation blocks submit as stale
Confirmation manifest summary contains the exact confirmation-record hash
Adaptive Grill evidence remains separate from final submission confirmation
Pre-1.7.5 assignments remain compatible and explicit legacy-not-required
V1.8.0 assignments require the exact task-bound Member version and build_id before init
Required AI dialogue collaboration cannot be skipped and has gate_effect none

known_boundaries: human_collaboration none only disables Grill; it never waives final submission confirmation for V1.7.5 tasks.
The registered human owner must explicitly supply the personal stance and confirm the displayed current body hash; AI cannot infer, invent, or sign this response.
The local explicit-human-owner record prevents contract and hash mismatches but is not cryptographic identity proof.
Member submission confirmation authorizes only the individual contribution submission and has gate_effect none.
Pre-1.7.5 tasks remain legacy-not-required and cannot be retroactively signed.
